label: Rhythm ID
back: The 5-question framework for reading any strip: rate, regularity, P waves, PR interval, QRS width. Everything else follows from these answers.

label: Cardiac arrest
back: Shockable-arm cadence: shock → 2 min CPR → rhythm check → shock again → epi after the 2nd shock, then amiodarone/lidocaine after the 3rd.

label: Epinephrine
back: Epinephrine 1 mg IV/IO every 3–5 minutes during cardiac arrest.

label: Amiodarone
back: 300 mg first dose, 150 mg second dose for refractory VF/pVT.

label: Lidocaine
back: 1–1.5 mg/kg first dose, 0.5–0.75 mg/kg second dose.

label: Atropine
front: One at a time, up to three
back: 1 mg IV every 3–5 min, maximum total dose 3 mg, for symptomatic bradycardia.

label: Adenosine
back: 6 mg rapid IV push + flush; 12 mg if no conversion. Very short half-life demands a fast push.

label: Bradycardia
front: 1, up to 3, then Pace or Push
back: Atropine to a max of 3 mg; beyond that, transcutaneous pacing and/or dopamine or epinephrine infusion.

label: Sync vs. unsync
front: Sync what's organized, shock what's chaos
back: Any organized QRS being cardioverted gets synchronized shock. VF and irregular wide-complex rhythms get unsynchronized defibrillation.

label: H's & T's
back: Hypovolemia, Hypoxia, Hydrogen ion, Hypo/Hyperkalemia, Hypothermia — Tension pneumothorax, Tamponade, Toxins, Thrombosis (pulmonary), Thrombosis (coronary).

label: Hyperkalemia
back: Calcium → Bicarbonate → Insulin+Glucose → Kayexalate/diuretics/Dialysis. Only the last step actually removes potassium.

label: ACS
back: Aspirin first and fast, Nitro if BP allows, Oxygen only if hypoxic, Morphine last resort for refractory pain.

label: Stroke
back: CT read within 20 min, door-to-needle within 60 min, stroke-unit admission within 3 hours.

label: Post-ROSC
back: Ventilation target, Investigate the cause, Temperature control (32–37.5°C), Arterial pressure (MAP ≥65), Level of glucose, Seizure/EEG watch.

label: Team dynamics
front: Say it, repeat it, confirm it
back: Closed-loop communication: leader gives the order, team member repeats it back, then confirms once it's done.
